title: THE DUSIT D2 HOTEL TERROR ATTACK
description: On January 15, 2019, at approximately 2:30 PM, four armed militants attacked the DusitD2 hotel complex in Nairobi's Westlands district. The attack began with an explosion in the parking lot, followed by gunfire as the attackers entered the hotel and office complex.

incident.title: THE DUSIT D2 HOTEL TERROR ATTACK
incident.background:
Incident Background
On January 15, 2019, at approximately 2:30 PM, four armed militants attacked the DusitD2 hotel complex in Nairobi's Westlands district. The attack began with an explosion in the parking lot, followed by gunfire as the attackers entered the hotel and office complex.

Incident Statistics:

21 fatalities (including civilians and security personnel)

28 injured survivors requiring medical evacuation

Approximately 700 people successfully evacuated

Duration of operation: Nearly 20 hours

Response time: First responders arrived within 10 minutes of the initial explosion

You are an EMT-Basic assigned to an ambulance stationed at Kenyatta National Hospital. The following dispatch message is received:

"MAJOR INCIDENT - ACTIVE TERROR ATTACK - DUSITD2 HOTEL, 14 RIVERSIDE DRIVE, WESTLANDS.

Multiple casualties reported. Explosion and active gunfire confirmed. Unknown number of injured. Multiple agencies responding including ATPU, GSU, KDF, and Kenya Red Cross.

Stage at the designated casualty collection point. Proceed with caution. Security forces are clearing the area. Do not enter the hot zone."

ems_response.title: Part 3: Mass Casualty Management
ems_response.content:
The situation escalates. More patients are being evacuated from the building. Approximately 30 additional casualties are being transported to the collection point. Security forces are reporting that the attack is still ongoing. Your ambulance can transport 2 patients at a time. Additional ambulances are 15-20 minutes away.

New arrivals include:

5 patients with blast injuries from the initial explosion

3 patients with gunshot wounds to the extremities

8 patients with smoke inhalation

2 pediatric patients (ages 6 and 8) with shrapnel injuries

12 patients with anxiety/panic attacks

1 pregnant woman with signs of labor (initiated by the stress)

The Kenya Red Cross triage officer requests your assistance in coordinating the evacuation plan.

🛑 STUDENT ACTION REQUIRED - PHASE 3
6. Resource Management & Transport Prioritization:
Develop a transport priority plan considering:

Available ambulances (your one ALS unit + 2 BLS units on scene)

Hospital destination selection (KNH, Aga Khan, Nairobi Hospital)

The "Golden Hour" concept for trauma patients

ems_response.title: Part 4: Lessons Learned - The Dusit Response
ems_response.content:
The response to the Dusit D2 attack was widely praised for its coordination and efficiency compared to the 2013 Westgate Mall attack.

What made the Dusit response different?

Unified Command Structure - All units reported to the GSU, eliminating the power struggles seen at Westgate

Improved Intelligence Sharing - The National Counter Terrorism Centre (NCTC) had been strengthened

Better Equipment and Training - Elite units had received enhanced training with foreign partners

Faster Perimeter Establishment - The area was cordoned off within minutes

Registration of Evacuees - Survivors were registered to track them and prevent attackers from escaping

Multi-Agency Coordination - KDF, NPS, ATPU, and GSU worked together effectively

Survival Statistics:

Westgate (2013): 67 deaths, 4 days operation

DusitD2 (2019): 21 deaths, 20 hours operation

🛑 STUDENT ACTION REQUIRED - PHASE 4
8. Reflection & Key Takeaways:

Your Response: (fill in)

______________________________________________________________________

2. How did improved incident command affect patient outcomes?
______________________________________________________________________

3. What are the challenges of providing care in a hostile environment?
______________________________________________________________________

4. How does START triage help manage mass casualty incidents?
______________________________________________________________________

title: KAKAMEGA PRIMARY SCHOOL STAMPEDE
description: On February 3, 2020, at approximately 5:00 PM, a tragic stampede occurred at Kakamega Primary School in Western Kenya, resulting in the deaths of 13 children.

incident.title: KAKAMEGA PRIMARY SCHOOL STAMPEDE
incident.background:
Incident Background
On February 3, 2020, at approximately 5:00 PM, a tragic stampede occurred at Kakamega Primary School in Western Kenya, resulting in the deaths of 13 children.

Incident Statistics:

13 children killed

39 students admitted to hospital

37 students treated and discharged

2 students in intensive care

Age of victims: Mostly grade five, aged 10-12

Time of incident: Around 5:00 PM (1400 GMT)

The Tragedy:

The stampede occurred as children were leaving their primary school. The police have launched an inquiry into what caused the crowd of students to panic, leading to the crush. Images broadcast by local media showed parents gathered in front of the emergency ward of a hospital in the town, waiting for news of their children.

Witness Accounts:

One of the children's mothers blamed the teachers: "Those who survived said they were running because there were teachers who were beating them, and that is why they were escaping and fell on each other."

Corporal punishment is banned in Kenya.

Government Response:

Kenya's Vice President William Ruto stated: "We are devastated by the tragedy that has hit Kakamega Primary School this evening. Our prayers, love and thoughts to the families and relatives of the victims of the misfortune."

Kenya Red Cross set up psychological support services and a "tracing desk" to help relatives locate potentially affected students.

Kakamega police chief David Kabena: "We have launched an investigation to establish what exactly happened."

Parliamentary Follow-up:

Senator Bonny Khalwale raised questions in the Senate asking for:

A list of the learners who lost their lives

When families will receive compensation awarded by courts

The amount due to each family

When families of three students and a teacher who died from water poisoning at Mukumu Girls' High School will receive compensation

activities.title: Incident Background
activities.content:
Incident Background
On February 3, 2020, at approximately 5:00 PM, a tragic stampede occurred at Kakamega Primary School in Western Kenya, resulting in the deaths of 13 children.

Incident Statistics:

13 children killed

39 students admitted to hospital

37 students treated and discharged

2 students in intensive care

Age of victims: Mostly grade five, aged 10-12

Time of incident: Around 5:00 PM (1400 GMT)

The Tragedy:

The stampede occurred as children were leaving their primary school. The police have launched an inquiry into what caused the crowd of students to panic, leading to the crush. Images broadcast by local media showed parents gathered in front of the emergency ward of a hospital in the town, waiting for news of their children.

Witness Accounts:

One of the children's mothers blamed the teachers: "Those who survived said they were running because there were teachers who were beating them, and that is why they were escaping and fell on each other."

Corporal punishment is banned in Kenya.

Government Response:

Kenya's Vice President William Ruto stated: "We are devastated by the tragedy that has hit Kakamega Primary School this evening. Our prayers, love and thoughts to the families and relatives of the victims of the misfortune."

Kenya Red Cross set up psychological support services and a "tracing desk" to help relatives locate potentially affected students.

Kakamega police chief David Kabena: "We have launched an investigation to establish what exactly happened."

Parliamentary Follow-up:

Senator Bonny Khalwale raised questions in the Senate asking for:

A list of the learners who lost their lives

When families will receive compensation awarded by courts

The amount due to each family

When families of three students and a teacher who died from water poisoning at Mukumu Girls' High School will receive compensation

activities.content:

Confirmed: Multiple pediatric casualties. Unknown number of injured. Students reportedly trampled.

Multi-agency response: County Disaster Response Teams, Kenya Red Cross, National Police Service, St John Ambulance.

Respond immediately. Be prepared for mass casualty triage of pediatric patients."

Incident Background
At approximately 12:30 AM on October 14, 2025, a Citi Hoppa bus was involved in a serious accident along Kenyatta Avenue near Serena Hotel in Nairobi.

Resources overwhelmed: "I have mobilised all available county ambulances, but they are not enough. If you have an ambulance, please come and assist us immediately"

Context of Road Safety Crisis:

Just days earlier, another accident occurred at Waruku

Nairobi County has only 20 ambulances - far short of the 50 required

Response times range from 20 to 60 minutes depending on traffic

activities.title: Incident Background
activities.content:
Incident Background
At approximately 12:30 AM on October 14, 2025, a Citi Hoppa bus was involved in a serious accident along Kenyatta Avenue near Serena Hotel in Nairobi.

Incident Statistics:

24 casualties admitted to Kenyatta National Hospital

11 females, 13 males among the casualties

Multiple patients were trapped under the bus

Resources overwhelmed: "I have mobilised all available county ambulances, but they are not enough. If you have an ambulance, please come and assist us immediately"

Context of Road Safety Crisis:

Just days earlier, another accident occurred at Waruku

Nairobi County has only 20 ambulances - far short of the 50 required

Response times range from 20 to 60 minutes depending on traffic

Proceed immediately. Coordinate with Kenya Red Cross at the scene."

incident.background:
Incident Background
On May 11, 2026, at approximately 8:00 PM, a four-storey building under construction collapsed in the Tom Mboya Estate, Kisumu.

Incident Statistics:

1 victim successfully rescued alive

Distress call: Received at 8:06 PM

Response time: 20 minutes (within international standards)

Rescue time: Victim rescued at 3:00 AM (7 hours after collapse)

Multiple agencies: Emergency Operations Centre, Fire Department, Kenya Red Cross, County Police, Ambulance Services

The Successful Response:
"The victim was successfully rescued at around 3 a.m. after hours of careful excavation and rescue efforts"

The Building Collapse Crisis (Kenya)
2026 Incidents in Nairobi alone:

January 2: Multi-storey building collapsed in South C estate, 2 guards killed

January 10: Building crumbled in Karen, 2 lives lost, several injured

February 10: Building collapsed near OTC, 6 workers rescued

The South C Incident: "Early assessments by Cabinet Secretary Geoffrey Ruku suggest the structure may have failed due to column overload. Officials say the building had been approved for 12 storeys but had reportedly reached between 14 and 16 storeys before it collapsed"

title: KAKAMEGA FUNERAL BUS CRASH
description: On August 8, 2025, a bus carrying mourners returning from a funeral crashed along the Kisumu-Kakamega Highway.

incident.title: KAKAMEGA FUNERAL BUS CRASH
incident.background:
Incident Background
On August 8, 2025, a bus carrying mourners returning from a funeral crashed along the Kisumu-Kakamega Highway.

Incident Statistics:

25 total deaths (21 at scene, 4 in hospital)

10 women, 10 men, 1 girl died at the scene

20 passengers injured (5 seriously)

All passengers from one family

Location: Near a roundabout on the Kisumu-Kakamega Highway

Vehicle: Secondary school bus being used for funeral transport

The Crash:

"The driver lost control, veered off the road and overturned into a ditch along the Kisumu-Kakamega Highway on Friday afternoon," a police report seen by the BBC says. The area is notorious for many deadly accidents.

According to Peter Maina, a regional traffic enforcement officer for Nyanza province, the driver lost control of the bus as it approached a roundabout at high speed and plunged into a ditch.

Government Response:

Kenya's Ministry of Health called for an "urgent blood drive" to help survivors

Kenyan President William Ruto called for authorities to quickly book "those responsible for any acts of negligence leading to the accident"

National Transport and Safety Authority (NTSA) will aid investigations

Context of Road Safety Crisis:

Road deaths in Kenya rose more than 20% between 2020-2021

In 2021, more than 4,500 people were killed and more than 16,000 injured

This area is notorious for many deadly accidents

Incident Background
On August 8, 2025, a bus carrying mourners returning from a funeral crashed along the Kisumu-Kakamega Highway.

Incident Statistics:

25 total deaths (21 at scene, 4 in hospital)

10 women, 10 men, 1 girl died at the scene

20 passengers injured (5 seriously)

All passengers from one family

Location: Near a roundabout on the Kisumu-Kakamega Highway

Vehicle: Secondary school bus being used for funeral transport

The Crash:

"The driver lost control, veered off the road and overturned into a ditch along the Kisumu-Kakamega Highway on Friday afternoon," a police report seen by the BBC says. The area is notorious for many deadly accidents.

According to Peter Maina, a regional traffic enforcement officer for Nyanza province, the driver lost control of the bus as it approached a roundabout at high speed and plunged into a ditch.

Government Response:

Kenya's Ministry of Health called for an "urgent blood drive" to help survivors

Kenyan President William Ruto called for authorities to quickly book "those responsible for any acts of negligence leading to the accident"

National Transport and Safety Authority (NTSA) will aid investigations

Context of Road Safety Crisis:

Road deaths in Kenya rose more than 20% between 2020-2021

In 2021, more than 4,500 people were killed and more than 16,000 injured

This area is notorious for many deadly accidents

incident.title: KAREN BUILDING COLLAPSE
incident.background:
Incident Background
On January 10, 2026, a residential building under construction collapsed along Ngong View Lane in Karen, Nairobi.

Incident Statistics:

2 deaths (severe crush injuries)

7 injured rescued and rushed to hospital

Location: Karen Ward, Lang'ata Sub-County

Time of collapse: During construction work

Cause of Collapse:

Preliminary investigations pointed to structural failure linked to poor workmanship and inadequate formwork.

"Preliminary investigations indicate the collapse was caused by structural failure resulting from poor workmanship and inadequate formwork. Initial findings show the use of sub-standard materials, where timber gum tree supports were used instead of appropriate steel props for the double-volume slab." - Nairobi CEC Member Patrick Mbogo

Project Details:

Developer: Moses A. Nyakiogora

Structural Engineer: Eng. Edward Kariuki

title: MICHAEL WAFULA - 5 HOURS, NO AMBULANCE
description: Michael Wafula did not die from the impact of the vehicle that struck him. He died from what came after: 30 calls, and not one of them reached anyone who could help.

incident.title: MICHAEL WAFULA - 5 HOURS, NO AMBULANCE
incident.background:
Incident Background
Michael Wafula did not die from the impact of the vehicle that struck him. He died from what came after: 30 calls, and not one of them reached anyone who could help.

The Sequence of Events:

9:47 AM - The Impact:
Wafula was walking along a road in Machakos County when a vehicle struck him and drove away. He was left on the tarmac, his body broken, his breathing shallow, and his consciousness slipping in and out. A passing motorist noticed him, checked his pulse and breathing, and took out his phone to start looking for help.

9:51 AM - The First Call:
A crowd gathered. Together with other motorists, they called the nearest public hospital. The line connected and rang. Nobody answered. They tried again. Same result.

9:58 AM - The Second Call:
They tried a different facility. A line connected, and a voice told them the hospital had no ambulance available, and that they should try another number. They asked for it, wrote it down, and called. It rang out. No response.

10:20 AM - The Emergency Line:
They found the county emergency line listed on a government website and called it. It did not connect. They called the national emergency toll-free numbers—999, 112, and 911—used to reach police, fire, or ambulance services countrywide. No one answered.

12:00 PM - More Numbers:
One call finally connected to a private ambulance service. When the caller explained his location and the nature of the accident, he was asked to pay a down payment of Sh300,000 before an ambulance would be dispatched and a bed secured. He did not have the money.

2:30 PM - His Breathing Changed:
Five hours had passed. Wafula's breathing became labored. They were still trying to reach family members who were now on their way. A school bus driver agreed to take him to the nearest hospital.

3:07 PM - The Breathing Stopped:
Wafula died on the way to the hospital, surrounded by strangers who had volunteered to rush him there.

title: WESTLANDS BUILDING COLLAPSE
description: On March 18, 2026, at approximately 7:30 PM, a building under construction partially collapsed in Westlands, Nairobi.

incident.title: WESTLANDS BUILDING COLLAPSE
incident.background:
Incident Background
On March 18, 2026, at approximately 7:30 PM, a building under construction partially collapsed in Westlands, Nairobi.

Incident Statistics:

1 fatality (Vincent Mokaya, assistant crane operator)

1 injured rescued

2 escaped unhurt

Total trapped: 4 workers

Building height: 22 floors when collapse occurred

Time of collapse: Around 8 PM

The Response:
The National Disaster Management Unit (NDMU) responded along with multiple agencies. The response operation was called off at about 3:30 AM on March 19, after successfully accounting for all persons.

Immediate Context:
This was the latest such incident in the construction industry. Hours earlier, a building under construction collapsed in Chebocho Sub-location, Kericho County, leaving one person dead and several others injured.

The Kericho incident triggered a multi-agency emergency response involving Kenya Red Cross, National Government Administrative Officers, and local rescue teams. Four people were successfully rescued and rushed to Kericho County Referral Hospital.

incident.title: NAIROBI PROTESTS MEDICAL RESPONSE
incident.background:
Incident Background
During the 2024 protests in Nairobi, Médecins Sans Frontières/Doctors Without Borders (MSF) contributed to the emergency response efforts.

The Response:

Abbas Bahola, an Emergency Supervisor with MSF, described the response:

"For the recent protests, we made plans to dispatch our ambulance and medical team, we made sure we had additional staff ready to respond to an influx of patients, and arranged referral possibilities in place for people who need more advanced medical care."

Medical Setup:

Ambulances went to the Central Business District (CBD) to join other actors

Medical post was set up by the Nairobi Emergency Operating Coordination Centre (EOC) at Jamia Mosque

Later shifted to Holy Family Basilica

Types of Injuries:
"Most of the patients we had seen had fractures, wounds, soft tissue injuries, and a few gunshot wounds, both from rubber and live bullets."

Challenges:

Access to medical sites became complicated

An ambulance was attacked by rowdy crowds while transporting patients

Staff were shaken, one staff got physically injured

Delay in care of patients on board

Role of MSF:
"We treat and discharge those with less severe injuries. For more severe cases, we stabilize the patients and refer them in our ambulances to Mama Lucy hospital or Kenyatta National Hospital."

MSF triaged 71 patients in the first two days.

title: RAILA ODINGA'S BODY RECEPTION - MEDICAL COVERAGE
description: On October 16, 2025, the Ministry of Health deployed 36 ambulances to provide emergency medical coverage during the arrival and reception of the body of the late Hon. Raila Amolo Odinga at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA).

incident.title: RAILA ODINGA'S BODY RECEPTION - MEDICAL COVERAGE
incident.background:
Incident Background
On October 16, 2025, the Ministry of Health deployed 36 ambulances to provide emergency medical coverage during the arrival and reception of the body of the late Hon. Raila Amolo Odinga at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA).

The Deployment:

The Principal Secretary for Medical Services Ouma Oluga said the deployment would ensure rapid medical response and safety for thousands of mourners expected to gather at the airport and along major routes in Nairobi.

Ambulance Specifications:

Each ambulance fully equipped with Basic Life Support (BLS) and Advanced Life Support (ALS) facilities

Staffed with a paramedic, nurse, and driver

Aimed to provide immediate treatment and facilitate safe evacuation

Resources Mobilized:

Kenya Red Cross

St. John Ambulance

Nairobi Disaster Service

Nairobi Emergency Services

Kenyatta National Hospital

Port Health ambulance on standby at JKIA

Extended Coverage:
Mobilization efforts were ongoing to extend full emergency medical coverage to Kisumu and Siaya Counties, where national mourning activities were expected to continue.

title: ST GABRIEL ISONGO SECONDARY SCHOOL - PARENT INVASION
description: On January 15, 2024, angry parents stormed St Gabriel Isongo Secondary School in Mumias East, Kakamega County, to protest and eject the principal from his office over alleged poor performance in the 2023 KCSE examination.

incident.title: ST GABRIEL ISONGO SECONDARY SCHOOL - PARENT INVASION
incident.background:
Incident Background
On January 15, 2024, angry parents stormed St Gabriel Isongo Secondary School in Mumias East, Kakamega County, to protest and eject the principal from his office over alleged poor performance in the 2023 KCSE examination.

The Incident:

Angry parents and students breached security and went straight to the principal's office before frog-marching him out of the school's compound.

The Accusations:

Accused principal of failing to uplift the school's academic standards

Alleged lack of commitment

School's mean score had dropped from 5 to 2 under the principal's leadership

The Aftermath:

TSC transferred 17 teachers to other institutions within Bungoma and Kakamega counties. Only 8 teachers - employed by the Board of Management - were left to manage the school.

KUPPET officials urged teachers not to resume work until security was addressed. Kakamega branch chairman Johnstone Wabuti questioned why none of the suspects had been apprehended.

Government Response:
Education Cabinet Secretary Ezekiel Machogu warned: "Going forward, the Ministry of Education will treat any invasion to schools to frog-march teachers out of their workstations as acts of crime that will prompt firm and decisive action from law enforcers."

title: NATIONAL EMS SYSTEM GAPS - AUDITOR GENERAL'S REPORT

incident.title: NATIONAL EMS SYSTEM GAPS - AUDITOR GENERAL'S REPORT
incident.background:
Background
The Auditor General's report, released in 2026, cited serious gaps in emergency medical care covering ambulance services, hospital emergency care, and public awareness of pre-hospital emergency services.

Key Findings:

Ambulance Availability:
"Vehicles recorded in government asset registers as operational ambulances are non-functional, grounded by mechanical failure, lacking fuel, or missing critical equipment. In some cases, ambulances have been found to have been out of service for months or years without repair or replacement."

Response Times:
The internationally accepted standard is 15-20 minutes. The audit found delays ranging from 20 minutes to a full hour.

Ambulance Equipment:
Ambulances that were empty, carrying no equipment at all.

Emergency Lines:
Calls in several instances not connecting to any operational dispatch function.

County Shortfalls:

Nairobi County: 20 of 50 required ambulances; response times 20-60 minutes

Mombasa: Response times up to 60 minutes

Kisii County: Worst response times alongside Mombasa

WHO Standard:
One ambulance per 70,000 to 100,000 people.

The Policy Gap:

"The Kenya Emergency Medical Care Policy 2020 to 2030 was signed and gazetted. It exists. It identifies the right priorities, calls for the right investments, and maps the right interventions. But a policy document is not a working ambulance. Whether it is being implemented with the consistency, funding, and accountability it requires is another matter entirely."

incident.title: THE STATE OF EMERGENCY CARE - SYSTEM FAILURES
incident.background:
Based on all case studies, the following systemic issues have been identified:

Ambulance Shortage: Nairobi County has only 20 of the 50 ambulances required

Response Time Failures: Delays ranging from 20 minutes to over 5 hours

Equipment Gaps: Ambulances "empty, carrying no equipment at all"

Emergency Line Failures: 999, 112, and 911 calls not connecting

Private Ambulance Barriers: Sh300,000 down payment required

Hospital Overload: Kenyatta National Hospital queue delays causing deaths

School Safety Failures: Locked dormitory doors, corporal punishment, fire safety gaps

Building Regulation Failures: Substandard materials, structural violations
